topic: Gemma 4 On-Device Deployment Guide: Run Google's Open Model Locally

key_points: Step-by-step local run instructions via Unsloth
Covers quantization options and VRAM optimization
Comprehensive specs, benchmarks, and model size breakdown
Hardware requirement tables by model variant
Covers Ollama, LM Studio, and Hugging Face Transformers setup
Free tier / no-cost local inference focus
Covers both Ollama and LM Studio GUI paths
Recommends quantization levels per hardware tier
Core ML and MLX Swift for iOS
Memory footprint table for E2B and E4B

stats.fact: Gemma 4 released April 2, 2026 under Apache 2.0 license with no commercial restrictions
Four model sizes: E2B (~2.3B effective params), E4B (~4.5B), 26B MoE (3.8B active of 26B total), 31B Dense
Context windows: 128K tokens for E2B/E4B; 256K tokens for 26B MoE and 31B
INT4 VRAM requirements: E2B ~1.5GB, E4B ~3GB, 26B MoE ~16GB, 31B Dense ~18GB
26B MoE achieves 85 tokens/second on consumer hardware (AMD Ryzen AI MAX+ 128GB)
Gemma 4 31B scores 85.2% on MMLU Pro, 81.8% on HumanEval, 89.2% on AIME 2026
Gemma 4 Arena AI ELO: #3 globally at 1452
31B model can be fine-tuned with as little as 16GB VRAM using QLoRA via Unsloth
E2B mobile footprint: ~1.3GB disk, 2-3GB RAM at runtime (Q4_K_M)
E4B mobile footprint: ~2.5GB disk, 4-5GB RAM at runtime (Q4_K_M)
Android Studio now supports Gemma 4 as the local model for agentic coding (announced April 2026)
Gemma 4 26B MoE activates only 3.8B parameters per token, delivering ~97% of 31B quality at 8x less compute

angles: Privacy-first local AI: Run Gemma 4 with sensitive code or data without any API calls leaving your machine
Cost elimination: Replace expensive per-token API costs with one-time GPU/electricity spend using Gemma 4 on-device
MoE efficiency explained: Why the 26B model's 3.8B active parameters make it the sweet spot for most developers
Mobile offline AI: Running Gemma 4 E2B/E4B fully offline on Android and iOS devices via LiteRT and MLX
Apache 2.0 advantage: No fine-tuning restrictions—train Gemma 4 on proprietary data, redistribute commercially
Hardware tiering guide: Matching Gemma 4 variants to specific hardware from 4GB RAM phones to 64GB Mac Ultras
Tool ecosystem comparison: Ollama vs LM Studio vs Hugging Face Transformers vs llama.cpp for local inference
